title: ledger-entry@1
description: Append-only decision-ledger entry: a decision, load-bearing assumption, or operator correction persisted with provenance for later re-anchoring. STRUCTURAL ONLY — the schema enforces required fields, identifier shapes, closed type/durability vocabularies, and the recurrent-correction failure-chain shape; it makes NO truth or authorization claim. In particular it cannot enforce the never-instruct boundary: a verdict-carrying-but-well-formed entry validates and is caught by human review, never claimed as schema-rejected (see README.md beside this file). Entries carry no trust-contract stamp and no valid_while predicate; freshness is revisit_when plus supersedes-chain re-anchoring, owned by the consumer.

synthetic.description: Optional example-marking flag for committed synthetic fixtures. Carries no semantics; consumers MUST NOT branch on it.

session.description: Optional harness session identifier for grouping. Carries no freshness semantics.

type.description: Closed vocabulary. If none fits, the moment is not a ledger moment.

statement.description: One sentence, outcome-shaped. A record of what was decided — never a verdict, never an instruction (human-enforced; not schema-checkable).

because.description: Provenance as resolvable coordinates (path:line, commit SHA, receipt id) — never unresolvable prose.

supersedes.description: Required, possibly empty. Ids of entries this one supersedes. Links must form an acyclic graph with a unique head and no dangling ids (store-level invariant, not schema-checkable); a malformed or unlinkable chain fails closed.

revisit_when.description: Falsification or review condition. Doubles as the store's GC horizon.

durability.description: Closed vocabulary. session-only entries are held only in session state and die with it; writing one emits a named durability-gap record consumed by the close-out rule.

recurrence_risk.description: Optional correction-only marker. true means the failure is likely to recur outside the corrected artifact and requires failure_chain.

failure_chain.description: Optional correction-only recurrent-failure analysis. Required when type=correction and recurrence_risk=true.
